v.CNV: 你们要称谢耶和华，因他本是良善的；他的慈爱永远长存。
v.ESV: Oh give thanks to the Lord , for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever!
v.GAE: 여호와께 감사하라 그는 선하시며 그 인자하심이 영원함이로다
v.KJV: O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
v.NASB: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, For His lovingkindness is everlasting.
v.NIV: Give thanks to the Lord , for he is good; his love endures forever.
v.NRSV: O give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever.
v.SAENEW: 주님께 감사드려라. 그는 선하시며, 그의 인자하심이 영원하다.
v.WLB: 여호와께 감사하라. 그분은 선하시고 그 인자하심이 영원하시다.

v.CNV: 愿耶和华救赎的子民这样称谢他；这民就是他从敌人的手里救赎出来，
v.ESV: Let the redeemed of the Lord say so, whom he has redeemed from trouble
v.GAE: 여호와의 속량을 받은 자들은 이같이 말할지어다 여호와께서 대적의 손에서 그들을 속량하사
v.KJV: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, whom he hath redeemed from the hand of the enemy;
v.NASB: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, Whom He has redeemed from the hand of the adversary
v.NIV: Let the redeemed of the Lord tell their story— those he redeemed from the hand of the foe,
v.NRSV: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, those he redeemed from trouble
v.SAENEW: 주님께 구원받은 사람들아, 대적의 손에서 구원받은 사람들아, 모두 주님께 감사드려라.
v.WLB: 여호와께서 구원하신 사람들은 이처럼 말해야 한다. 하나님께서 적들의 손에서 구원해

v.CNV: 把他们从各地，就是从东从西、从南（“南”原文作“海”）从北，招聚回来的。
v.ESV: and gathered in from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.
v.GAE: 동서 남북 각 지방에서부터 모으셨도다
v.KJV: And gathered them out of the lands, from the east, and from the west, from the north, and from the south.
v.NASB: And gathered from the lands, From the east and from the west, From the north and from the south.
v.NIV: those he gathered from the lands, from east and west, from north and south.
v.NRSV: and gathered in from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.
v.SAENEW: 동서 남북 사방에서, 주님께서 모아들이신 사람들아, 모두 주님께 감사드려라.
v.WLB: 그들을 땅에서, 동서남북에서 모으셨다.

v.CNV: 他们有些人在旷野、在荒漠飘流，寻不见路往可居住的城巿。
v.ESV: Some wandered in desert wastes, finding no way to a city to dwell in;
v.GAE: 그들이 광야 사막 길에서 방황하며 거주할 성읍을 찾지 못하고
v.KJV: They wandered in the wilderness in a solitary way; they found no city to dwell in.
v.NASB: They wandered in the wilderness in a desert region; They did not find a way to an inhabited city.
v.NIV: Some wandered in desert wastelands, finding no way to a city where they could settle.
v.NRSV: Some wandered in desert wastes, finding no way to an inhabited town;
v.SAENEW: 어떤 이들은 광야의 사막에서 길을 잃고, 사람이 사는 성읍으로 가는 길을 찾지 못했으며,
v.WLB: 그들은 정착할 성을 찾지 못하고 광야에서 엉뚱한 길로 다니며 방황하다가

v.CNV: 他们又饥又渴，心灵疲弱。
v.ESV: hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
v.GAE: 주리고 목이 말라 그들의 영혼이 그들 안에서 피곤하였도다
v.KJV: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.
v.NASB: They were hungry and thirsty; Their soul fainted within them.
v.NIV: They were hungry and thirsty, and their lives ebbed away.
v.NRSV: hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
v.SAENEW: 배고프고 목이 말라, 기력이 다 빠지기도 하였다.
v.WLB: 굶주리고 목이 말라 영혼의 기력을 잃은 일이 있었다.

v.CNV: 他们在急难中呼求耶和华，耶和华就救他们脱离困苦；
v.ESV: Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
v.GAE: 이에 그들이 근심 중에 여호와께 부르짖으매 그들의 고통에서 건지시고
v.KJV: Then they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distresses.
v.NASB: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He delivered them out of their distresses.
v.NIV: Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
v.NRSV: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress;
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그들이 그 고난 가운데서 주님께 부르짖을 때에, 주님께서는 그들을 그 고통에서 건지시고,
v.WLB: 그 고통 가운데 그들이 여호와께 부르짖었더니 그분이 그들을 고통에서 건져 주시고

v.CNV: 领他们走正直的路，使他们去到可居住的城巿。
v.ESV: He led them by a straight way till they reached a city to dwell in.
v.GAE: 또 바른 길로 인도하사 거주할 성읍에 이르게 하셨도다
v.KJV: And he led them forth by the right way, that they might go to a city of habitation.
v.NASB: He led them also by a straight way, To go to an inhabited city.
v.NIV: He led them by a straight way to a city where they could settle.
v.NRSV: he led them by a straight way, until they reached an inhabited town.
v.SAENEW: 바른길로 들어서게 하셔서, 사람이 사는 성읍으로 들어가게 하셨다.
v.WLB: 바른 길로 인도하시어 정착할 성으로 들어가게 해 주셨다.

v.CNV: 愿人因耶和华的慈爱，和他向世人所行的奇事称谢他。
v.ESV: Let them thank the Lord for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
v.GAE: 여호와의 인자하심과 인생에게 행하신 기적으로 말미암아 그를 찬송할지로다
v.KJV: Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!
v.NASB: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
v.NIV: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for mankind,
v.NRSV: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to humankind.
v.SAENEW: 주님의 인자하심을 감사하여라. 사람들에게 베푸신 주님의 놀라운 구원을 감사하여라.
v.WLB: 여호와의 선하심과 사람의 자손들에게 하신 그 놀라운 일들로 인해 사람들은 주를 찬양하라.

v.CNV: 因为他使干渴的人得到满足，又使饥饿的人饱享美物。
v.ESV: For he satisfies the longing soul, and the hungry soul he fills with good things.
v.GAE: 그가 사모하는 영혼에게 만족을 주시며 주린 영혼에게 좋은 것으로 채워주심이로다
v.KJV: For he satisfieth the longing soul, and filleth the hungry soul with goodness.
v.NASB: For He has satisfied the thirsty soul, And the hungry soul He has filled with what is good.
v.NIV: for he satisfies the thirsty and fills the hungry with good things.
v.NRSV: For he satisfies the thirsty, and the hungry he fills with good things.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서는 목마른 사람에게 물을 실컷 마시게 하시고, 배고픈 사람에게 좋은 음식을 마음껏 먹게 해주셨다.
v.WLB: 그분이 목마른 영혼들에게 넉넉하게 물을 주시고 굶주린 영혼들에게 좋은 것으로 채워 주셨다.

v.CNV: 他们有些人坐在黑暗和死荫里，被困苦和铁链捆锁着；
v.ESV: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, prisoners in affliction and in irons,
v.GAE: 사람이 흑암과 사망의 그늘에 앉으며 곤고와 쇠사슬에 매임은
v.KJV: Such as sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, being bound in affliction and iron;
v.NASB: There were those who dwelt in darkness and in the shadow of death, Prisoners in misery and chains,
v.NIV: Some sat in darkness, in utter darkness, prisoners suffering in iron chains,
v.NRSV: Some sat in darkness and in gloom, prisoners in misery and in irons,
v.SAENEW: 사람이 어둡고 캄캄한 곳에서 살며, 고통과 쇠사슬에 묶이는 것은,
v.WLB: 그들이 어둠 속에, 죽음의 그림자 가운데 앉아 쇠사슬에 묶인 채 고난에 빠진 일도 있었다.

v.CNV: 因为他们违背了　神的话，藐视了至高者的旨意。
v.ESV: for they had rebelled against the words of God, and spurned the counsel of the Most High.
v.GAE: 하나님의 말씀을 거역하며 지존자의 뜻을 멸시함이라
v.KJV: Because they rebelled against the words of God, and contemned the counsel of the most High:
v.NASB: Because they had rebelled against the words of God And spurned the counsel of the Most High.
v.NIV: because they rebelled against God’s commands and despised the plans of the Most High.
v.NRSV: for they had rebelled against the words of God, and spurned the counsel of the Most High.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 하나님의 말씀을 거역하고, 가장 높으신 분의 뜻을 저버렸기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 하나님의 말씀을 거역하고 지극히 높으신 분의 가르침을 무시했기 때문이다.

v.CNV: 所以　神用苦难治服他们的心；他们跌倒，也没有人帮助。
v.ESV: So he bowed their hearts down with hard labor; they fell down, with none to help.
v.GAE: 그러므로 그가 고통을 주어 그들의 마음을 겸손하게 하셨으니 그들이 엎드러져도 돕는 자가 없었도다
v.KJV: Therefore he brought down their heart with labour; they fell down, and there was none to help.
v.NASB: Therefore He humbled their heart with labor; They stumbled and there was none to help.
v.NIV: So he subjected them to bitter labor; they stumbled, and there was no one to help.
v.NRSV: Their hearts were bowed down with hard labor; they fell down, with no one to help.
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 주님께서는 그들의 마음에 고통을 주셔서 그들을 낮추셨으니, 그들이 비틀거려도 돕는 사람이 없었다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그분이 그들의 마음에 어려움을 주어 낮추셨던 것이다. 그들이 넘어져도 돕는 사람 하나 없었다.

v.CNV: 他们在急难中呼求耶和华，耶和华就拯救他们脱离困苦。
v.ESV: Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
v.GAE: 이에 그들이 그 환난 중에 여호와께 부르짖으매 그들의 고통에서 구원하시되
v.KJV: Then they cried unto the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses.
v.NASB: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
v.NIV: Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble, and he saved them from their distress.
v.NRSV: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them from their distress;
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그들이 고난 가운데서 주님께 부르짖을 때에, 그들을 그 곤경에서 구원해 주셨다.
v.WLB: 그때 그들이 그 고통 가운데서 여호와께 부르짖었더니 그분이 그들을 구원해 주셨다.

v.CNV: 他从黑暗里和死荫中把他们领出来，弄断他们的锁链。
v.ESV: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and burst their bonds apart.
v.GAE: 흑암과 사망의 그늘에서 인도하여 내시고 그들의 얽어 맨 줄을 끊으셨도다
v.KJV: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and brake their bands in sunder.
v.NASB: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death And broke their bands apart.
v.NIV: He brought them out of darkness, the utter darkness, and broke away their chains.
v.NRSV: he brought them out of darkness and gloom, and broke their bonds asunder.
v.SAENEW: 어둡고 캄캄한 데서 건져 주시고, 그들을 얽어 맨 사슬을 끊어 주셨다.
v.WLB: 그분이 그들을 어둠에서, 죽음의 그림자 속에서 끌어내시고 그들을 묶고 있던 것을 끊으셨다.

v.CNV: 愿人因耶和华的慈爱，和他向世人所行的奇事称谢他。
v.ESV: Let them thank the Lord for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
v.GAE: 여호와의 인자하심과 인생에게 행하신 기적으로 말미암아 그를 찬송할지로다
v.KJV: Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!
v.NASB: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
v.NIV: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for mankind,
v.NRSV: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to humankind.
v.SAENEW: 주님의 인자하심을 감사하여라. 사람에게 베푸신 주님의 놀라운 구원을 감사하여라.
v.WLB: 여호와의 변함없는 사랑과 사람의 자손들에게 하신 그 놀라운 일들을 생각하며 여호와께 감사하라.

v.CNV: 因为他打破了铜门，砍断了铁闩。
v.ESV: For he shatters the doors of bronze and cuts in two the bars of iron.
v.GAE: 그가 놋문을 깨뜨리시며 쇠빗장을 꺾으셨음이로다
v.KJV: For he hath broken the gates of brass, and cut the bars of iron in sunder.
v.NASB: For He has shattered gates of bronze And cut bars of iron asunder.
v.NIV: for he breaks down gates of bronze and cuts through bars of iron.
v.NRSV: For he shatters the doors of bronze, and cuts in two the bars of iron.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 놋대문을 부수시고, 쇠빗장을 깨뜨리셨기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 이는 주께서 청동으로 된 문을 허무셨고 철빗장을 잘라 버리셨기 때문이다.

v.CNV: 他们有些人因自己的过犯成了愚妄人，有人受苦是因为自己的罪孽。
v.ESV: Some were fools through their sinful ways, and because of their iniquities suffered affliction;
v.GAE: 미련한 자들은 그들의 죄악의 길을 따르고 그들의 악을 범하기 때문에 고난을 받아
v.KJV: Fools because of their transgression, and because of their iniquities, are afflicted.
v.NASB: Fools, because of their rebellious way, And because of their iniquities, were afflicted.
v.NIV: Some became fools through their rebellious ways and suffered affliction because of their iniquities.
v.NRSV: Some were sick through their sinful ways, and because of their iniquities endured affliction;
v.SAENEW: 어리석은 자들은, 반역의 길을 걷고 죄악을 저지르다가 고난을 받아
v.WLB: 어리석은 사람들이 반항하고 죄악을 저질렀기 때문에 곤란을 겪게 된 일도 있었다.

v.CNV: 他们厌恶各样食物，就临近死门。
v.ESV: they loathed any kind of food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
v.GAE: 그들은 그들의 모든 음식물을 싫어하게 되어 사망의 문에 이르렀도다
v.KJV: Their soul abhorreth all manner of meat; and they draw near unto the gates of death.
v.NASB: Their soul abhorred all kinds of food, And they drew near to the gates of death.
v.NIV: They loathed all food and drew near the gates of death.
v.NRSV: they loathed any kind of food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
v.SAENEW: 밥맛까지 잃었으니, 이미 죽음의 문턱에까지 이르렀다.
v.WLB: 그들의 영혼이 모든 음식을 거부하고 죽음의 문턱까지 이르렀을 그때,

v.CNV: 于是他们在急难中呼求耶和华，他就拯救他们脱离困苦。
v.ESV: Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
v.GAE: 이에 그들이 그들의 고통 때문에 여호와께 부르짖으매 그가 그들의 고통에서 그들을 구원하시되
v.KJV: Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, and he saveth them out of their distresses.
v.NASB: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
v.NIV: Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble, and he saved them from their distress.
v.NRSV: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he saved them from their distress;
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 그들이 고난 가운데서 주님께 부르짖으니, 주님께서 그들을 곤경에서 구원해 주셨다.
v.WLB: 그 고통 가운데서 여호와께 부르짖었더니 그분이 그들을 구원해 주셨다.

v.CNV: 他发出说话医治他们，搭救他们脱离死亡。
v.ESV: He sent out his word and healed them, and delivered them from their destruction.
v.GAE: 그가 그의 말씀을 보내어 그들을 고치시고 위험한 지경에서 건지시는도다
v.KJV: He sent his word, and healed them, and delivered them from their destructions.
v.NASB: He sent His word and healed them, And delivered them from their destructions.
v.NIV: He sent out his word and healed them; he rescued them from the grave.
v.NRSV: he sent out his word and healed them, and delivered them from destruction.
v.SAENEW: 단 한 마디 말씀으로 그들을 고쳐 주셨고, 그들을 멸망의 구렁에서 끌어내어 주셨다.
v.WLB: 그분이 말씀을 보내 그들을 고쳐 주시고 그들을 파멸에서 구해 주셨다.

v.CNV: 愿人因耶和华的慈爱，因他向世人所行的奇事称谢他。
v.ESV: Let them thank the Lord for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
v.GAE: 여호와의 인자하심과 인생에게 행하신 기적으로 말미암아 그를 찬송할지로다
v.KJV: Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!
v.NASB: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
v.NIV: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for mankind.
v.NRSV: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to humankind.
v.SAENEW: 주님의 인자하심을 감사하여라. 사람에게 베푸신 주님의 놀라운 구원을 감사하여라.
v.WLB: 여호와의 선하심과 사람의 자손들에게 하신 그 놀라운 일들을 두고 여호와께 감사하라.

v.CNV: 愿他们以感谢为祭献给他，欢欣地述说他的作为。
v.ESV: And let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, and tell of his deeds in songs of joy!
v.GAE: 감사제를 드리며 노래하여 그가 행하신 일을 선포할지로다
v.KJV: And let them sacrifice the sacrifices of thanksgiving, and declare his works with rejoicing.
v.NASB: Let them also offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, And tell of His works with joyful singing.
v.NIV: Let them sacrifice thank offerings and tell of his works with songs of joy.
v.NRSV: And let them offer thanksgiving sacrifices, and tell of his deeds with songs of joy.
v.SAENEW: 감사의 제물을 드리고, 주님이 이루신 일을 즐거운 노래로 널리 퍼뜨려라.
v.WLB: 감사제물을 바치고 기쁨의 노래로 그분이 하신 일들을 선포하는 것이 마땅하다.

v.CNV: 他们有些人坐船出海，在大海上往来经商。
v.ESV: Some went down to the sea in ships, doing business on the great waters;
v.GAE: 배들을 바다에 띄우며 큰 물에서 일을 하는 자는
v.KJV: They that go down to the sea in ships, that do business in great waters;
v.NASB: Those who go down to the sea in ships, Who do business on great waters;
v.NIV: Some went out on the sea in ships; they were merchants on the mighty waters.
v.NRSV: Some went down to the sea in ships, doing business on the mighty waters;
v.SAENEW: 배를 타고 바다로 내려가서, 큰 물을 헤쳐 가면서 장사하는 사람들은,
v.WLB: 배를 타고 바다에 나가 큰물에서 장사하는 사람들이 있는데

v.CNV: 他们见过耶和华的作为，和他在深海中所行的奇事。
v.ESV: they saw the deeds of the Lord , his wondrous works in the deep.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 행하신 일들과 그의 기이한 일들을 깊은 바다에서 보나니
v.KJV: These see the works of the LORD, and his wonders in the deep.
v.NASB: They have seen the works of the LORD, And His wonders in the deep.
v.NIV: They saw the works of the Lord , his wonderful deeds in the deep.
v.NRSV: they saw the deeds of the LORD, his wondrous works in the deep.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 하신 행사를 보고, 깊은 바다에서 일으키신 놀라운 기적을 본다.
v.WLB: 그들은 여호와께서 하신 일을, 깊은 물에서 그분이 하신 놀라운 일들을 본다.

v.CNV: 他一吩咐，狂风就刮起，海中的波浪也高扬。
v.ESV: For he commanded and raised the stormy wind, which lifted up the waves of the sea.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 명령하신즉 광풍이 일어나 바다 물결을 일으키는도다
v.KJV: For he commandeth, and raiseth the stormy wind, which lifteth up the waves thereof.
v.NASB: For He spoke and raised up a stormy wind, Which lifted up the waves of the sea.
v.NIV: For he spoke and stirred up a tempest that lifted high the waves.
v.NRSV: For he commanded and raised the stormy wind, which lifted up the waves of the sea.
v.SAENEW: 그는 말씀으로 큰 폭풍을 일으키시고, 물결을 산더미처럼 쌓으신다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 명령해 큰바람을 일으키시면 큰 물결이 일어난다.

v.CNV: 他们上到天上，下到深渊，他们的心因危难而惊慌。
v.ESV: They mounted up to heaven; they went down to the depths; their courage melted away in their evil plight;
v.GAE: 그들이 하늘로 솟구쳤다가 깊은 곳으로 내려가나니 그 위험 때문에 그들의 영혼이 녹는도다
v.KJV: They mount up to the heaven, they go down again to the depths: their soul is melted because of trouble.
v.NASB: They rose up to the heavens, they went down to the depths; Their soul melted away in their misery.
v.NIV: They mounted up to the heavens and went down to the depths; in their peril their courage melted away.
v.NRSV: They mounted up to heaven, they went down to the depths; their courage melted away in their calamity;
v.SAENEW: 배들은 하늘 높이 떠올랐다가 깊은 바다로 떨어진다. 그런 위기에서 그들은 얼이 빠지고 간담이 녹는다.
v.WLB: 그 물결이 하늘까지 올라갔다가 깊은 곳에 내려가기도 할 때 그들의 영혼이 걱정으로 녹아 없어진다.

v.CNV: 他们摇摇晃晃，东倒西歪，好像醉酒的人；他们的一切智慧都没有用了。
v.ESV: they reeled and staggered like drunken men and were at their wits' end.
v.GAE: 그들이 이리저리 구르며 취한 자 같이 비틀거리니 그들의 모든 지각이 혼돈 속에 빠지는도다
v.KJV: They reel to and fro, and stagger like a drunken man, and are at their wit’s end.
v.NASB: They reeled and staggered like a drunken man, And were at their wits' end.
v.NIV: They reeled and staggered like drunkards; they were at their wits’ end.
v.NRSV: they reeled and staggered like drunkards, and were at their wits’ end.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 모두 술 취한 사람처럼 비틀거리며 흔들리니, 그들의 지혜가 모두 쓸모 없이 된다.
v.WLB: 그들이 이리저리 구르고 술 취한 것처럼 비틀거리며 어쩔 줄 모른다.

v.CNV: 于是他们在急难中呼求耶和华，他就拯救他们脱离困苦。
v.ESV: Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
v.GAE: 이에 그들이 그들의 고통 때문에 여호와께 부르짖으매 그가 그들의 고통에서 그들을 인도하여 내시고
v.KJV: Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, and he bringeth them out of their distresses.
v.NASB: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, And He brought them out of their distresses.
v.NIV: Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble, and he brought them out of their distress.
v.NRSV: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he brought them out from their distress;
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그들이 고난 가운데서 주님께 부르짖을 때에, 그들을 곤경에서 벗어나게 해주신다.
v.WLB: 그때 그들이 그 고통 가운데 여호와께 부르짖으면 그분께서 그들을 고통 가운데서 끌어내 주실 것이다.

v.CNV: 他使狂风止息，海浪就平静无声。
v.ESV: He made the storm be still, and the waves of the sea were hushed.
v.GAE: 광풍을 고요하게 하사 물결도 잔잔하게 하시는도다
v.KJV: He maketh the storm a calm, so that the waves thereof are still.
v.NASB: He caused the storm to be still, So that the waves of the sea were hushed.
v.NIV: He stilled the storm to a whisper; the waves of the sea were hushed.
v.NRSV: he made the storm be still, and the waves of the sea were hushed.
v.SAENEW: 폭풍이 잠잠해지고, 물결도 잔잔해진다.
v.WLB: 하나님께서 폭풍을 잠잠하게 하시면 바다 물결도 잔잔해진다.

v.CNV: 风平浪静了，他们就欢喜；他引领他们到他们所愿去的港口。
v.ESV: Then they were glad that the waters were quiet, and he brought them to their desired haven.
v.GAE: 그들이 평온함으로 말미암아 기뻐하는 중에 여호와께서 그들이 바라는 항구로 인도하시는도다
v.KJV: Then are they glad because they be quiet; so he bringeth them unto their desired haven.
v.NASB: Then they were glad because they were quiet, So He guided them to their desired haven.
v.NIV: They were glad when it grew calm, and he guided them to their desired haven.
v.NRSV: Then they were glad because they had quiet, and he brought them to their desired haven.
v.SAENEW: 사방이 조용해지니 모두들 기뻐하고, 주님은 그들이 바라는 항구로 그들을 인도하여 주신다.
v.WLB: 물결이 가라앉으면 그들이 기뻐하고 여호와께서는 그들을 원하는 항구까지 인도해 주신다.

v.CNV: 愿人因耶和华的慈爱，和他向世人所行的奇事称谢他。
v.ESV: Let them thank the Lord for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
v.GAE: 여호와의 인자하심과 인생에게 행하신 기적으로 말미암아 그를 찬송할지로다
v.KJV: Oh that men would praise the LORD for his goodness, and for his wonderful works to the children of men!
v.NASB: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
v.NIV: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love and his wonderful deeds for mankind.
v.NRSV: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wonderful works to humankind.
v.SAENEW: 주님의 인자하심을 감사하여라. 사람에게 베푸신 주님의 놀라운 구원을 감사하여라.
v.WLB: 오 사람들아, 여호와의 선하심과 사람의 자손들에게 하신 그 놀라운 일들을 두고 여호와께 감사하라.

v.CNV: 愿他们在众民的会中尊崇他，在长老的集会中赞美他。
v.ESV: Let them extol him in the congregation of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
v.GAE: 백성의 모임에서 그를 높이며 장로들의 자리에서 그를 찬송할지로다
v.KJV: Let them exalt him also in the congregation of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
v.NASB: Let them extol Him also in the congregation of the people, And praise Him at the seat of the elders.
v.NIV: Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people and praise him in the council of the elders.
v.NRSV: Let them extol him in the congregation of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
v.SAENEW: 백성이 모인 가운데서 그분을 기려라. 장로들이 모인 곳에서 그분을 찬양하여라.
v.WLB: 집회에서 그분을 높이고 원로들의 공회에서 그분을 찬양하라.

v.CNV: 他使江河变为旷野，使水泉变为干旱无水之地。
v.ESV: He turns rivers into a desert, springs of water into thirsty ground,
v.GAE: 여호와께서는 강이 변하여 광야가 되게 하시며 샘이 변하여 마른 땅이 되게 하시며
v.KJV: He turneth rivers into a wilderness, and the watersprings into dry ground;
v.NASB: He changes rivers into a wilderness And springs of water into a thirsty ground;
v.NIV: He turned rivers into a desert, flowing springs into thirsty ground,
v.NRSV: He turns rivers into a desert, springs of water into thirsty ground,
v.SAENEW: 주님께서는 강들을 사막으로 만드시며, 물이 솟는 샘들을 마른 땅이 되게 하시며,
v.WLB: 그분이 강물을 광야로, 샘물을 마른 땅으로 변하게 하시고

v.CNV: 他使肥沃的土地变为咸田，都因住在那里的居民的邪恶。
v.ESV: a fruitful land into a salty waste, because of the evil of its inhabitants.
v.GAE: 그 주민의 악으로 말미암아 옥토가 변하여 염전이 되게 하시며
v.KJV: A fruitful land into barrenness, for the wickedness of them that dwell therein.
v.NASB: A fruitful land into a salt waste, Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
v.NIV: and fruitful land into a salt waste, because of the wickedness of those who lived there.
v.NRSV: a fruitful land into a salty waste, because of the wickedness of its inhabitants.
v.SAENEW: 그 곳에서 사는 사람들의 죄악 때문에, 옥토를 소금밭이 되게 하신다.
v.WLB: 비옥한 땅을 소금밭으로 변하게 하시는 것은 그곳에 사는 사람들이 악하기 때문이다.

v.CNV: 他使旷野变为池塘，使旱地变为水泉。
v.ESV: He turns a desert into pools of water, a parched land into springs of water.
v.GAE: 또 광야가 변하여 못이 되게 하시며 마른 땅이 변하여 샘물이 되게 하시고
v.KJV: He turneth the wilderness into a standing water, and dry ground into watersprings.
v.NASB: He changes a wilderness into a pool of water And a dry land into springs of water;
v.NIV: He turned the desert into pools of water and the parched ground into flowing springs;
v.NRSV: He turns a desert into pools of water, a parched land into springs of water.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 주님께서는 사막을 연못으로 만드시며, 마른 땅을 물이 솟는 샘으로 만드시고,
v.WLB: 그분이 광야를 흐르는 물로, 마른 땅을 샘물로 바꾸시며

v.CNV: 他使饥饿的人住在那里，好让他们建造可以居住的城巿。
v.ESV: And there he lets the hungry dwell, and they establish a city to live in;
v.GAE: 주린 자들로 거기에 살게 하사 그들이 거주할 성읍을 준비하게 하시고
v.KJV: And there he maketh the hungry to dwell, that they may prepare a city for habitation;
v.NASB: And there He makes the hungry to dwell, So that they may establish an inhabited city,
v.NIV: there he brought the hungry to live, and they founded a city where they could settle.
v.NRSV: And there he lets the hungry live, and they establish a town to live in;
v.SAENEW: 굶주린 사람들로 거기에 살게 하시어, 그들이 거기에다 사람 사는 성읍을 세우게 하시고,
v.WLB: 거기에 굶주린 사람들을 데려와 살게 하시며 그들이 살 성읍을 준비하게 하신다.

v.CNV: 他们耕种田地，栽种葡萄园，得到丰盛的收成。
v.ESV: they sow fields and plant vineyards and get a fruitful yield.
v.GAE: 밭에 파종하며 포도원을 재배하여 풍성한 소출을 거두게 하시며
v.KJV: And sow the fields, and plant vineyards, which may yield fruits of increase.
v.NASB: And sow fields and plant vineyards, And gather a fruitful harvest.
v.NIV: They sowed fields and planted vineyards that yielded a fruitful harvest;
v.NRSV: they sow fields, and plant vineyards, and get a fruitful yield.
v.SAENEW: 밭에 씨를 뿌리며 포도원을 일구어서, 풍성한 소출을 거두게 하시며,
v.WLB: 밭에 씨를 뿌리고 포도원을 만들어 많은 수확을 얻게 하신다.

v.CNV: 他又赐福给他们，使他们人口众多，也没有使他们的牲畜减少。
v.ESV: By his blessing they multiply greatly, and he does not let their livestock diminish.
v.GAE: 또 복을 주사 그들이 크게 번성하게 하시고 그의 가축이 감소하지 아니하게 하실지라도
v.KJV: He blesseth them also, so that they are multiplied greatly; and suffereth not their cattle to decrease.
v.NASB: Also He blesses them and they multiply greatly, And He does not let their cattle decrease.
v.NIV: he blessed them, and their numbers greatly increased, and he did not let their herds diminish.
v.NRSV: By his blessing they multiply greatly, and he does not let their cattle decrease.
v.SAENEW: 또 그들에게 복을 주시어, 그들이 크게 번성하게 하시고, 가축이 줄어들지 않게 하신다.
v.WLB: 또 그들에게 복을 주어 그들의 수가 크게 늘어나게 하시고 그들의 가축 떼가 줄지 않게 하신다.

v.CNV: 以后他们因为压迫、患难、愁苦，就人数减少，降为卑微。
v.ESV: When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, evil, and sorrow,
v.GAE: 다시 압박과 재난과 우환을 통하여 그들의 수를 줄이시며 낮추시는도다
v.KJV: Again, they are minished and brought low through oppression, affliction, and sorrow.
v.NASB: When they are diminished and bowed down Through oppression, misery and sorrow,
v.NIV: Then their numbers decreased, and they were humbled by oppression, calamity and sorrow;
v.NRSV: When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, trouble, and sorrow,
v.SAENEW: 그들이 억압과 고난과 걱정 근심 때문에 수가 줄어들고 비천해질 때에,
v.WLB: 그리고 다시 억압과 재앙과 슬픔으로 그들의 수가 줄게 해 그들을 낮추신다.

v.CNV: 他使权贵受羞辱，使他们在荒废无路的地方飘流。
v.ESV: he pours contempt on princes and makes them wander in trackless wastes;
v.GAE: 여호와께서 고관들에게는 능욕을 쏟아 부으시고 길 없는 황야에서 유리하게 하시나
v.KJV: He poureth contempt upon princes, and causeth them to wander in the wilderness, where there is no way.
v.NASB: He pours contempt upon princes And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
v.NIV: he who pours contempt on nobles made them wander in a trackless waste.
v.NRSV: he pours contempt on princes and makes them wander in trackless wastes;
v.SAENEW: 주님께서는 높은 자들에게 능욕을 부으시고, 그들을 길 없는 황무지에서 헤매게 하셨지만,
v.WLB: 귀족들에게는 모욕을 주시고 길이 아닌 곳에서, 광야 가운데서 방황하게 하신다.

v.CNV: 他却把贫穷的人安置在高处，免受苦难，使他的家族繁衍，好像羊群。
v.ESV: but he raises up the needy out of affliction and makes their families like flocks.
v.GAE: 궁핍한 자는 그의 고통으로부터 건져 주시고 그의 가족을 양 떼 같이 지켜 주시나니
v.KJV: Yet setteth he the poor on high from affliction, and maketh him families like a flock.
v.NASB: But He sets the needy securely on high away from affliction, And makes his families like a flock.
v.NIV: But he lifted the needy out of their affliction and increased their families like flocks.
v.NRSV: but he raises up the needy out of distress, and makes their families like flocks.
v.SAENEW: 가난한 사람은 그 고달픔에서 벗어나게 해주시고, 그 가족을 양 떼처럼 번성하게 하셨다.
v.WLB: 그러나 궁핍한 사람들은 그 어려움에서 높이 드시고 그 가족들을 양 떼처럼 늘려 주신다.

v.CNV: 正直的人看见了，就欢喜；但所有邪恶的人都必闭口无言。
v.ESV: The upright see it and are glad, and all wickedness shuts its mouth.
v.GAE: 정직한 자는 보고 기뻐하며 모든 사악한 자는 자기 입을 봉하리로다
v.KJV: The righteous shall see it, and rejoice: and all iniquity shall stop her mouth.
v.NASB: The upright see it and are glad; But all unrighteousness shuts its mouth.
v.NIV: The upright see and rejoice, but all the wicked shut their mouths.
v.NRSV: The upright see it and are glad; and all wickedness stops its mouth.
v.SAENEW: 정직한 사람은 이것을 보고 즐거워하고, 사악한 사람은 말문이 막힐 것이다.
v.WLB: 의인들은 그것을 보고 기뻐할 것이요 악인들은 다 그 입을 다물 것이다.

v.CNV: 凡有智慧的，愿他留意这些事；愿他思想耶和华的慈爱。
v.ESV: Whoever is wise, let him attend to these things; let them consider the steadfast love of the Lord .
v.GAE: 지혜 있는 자들은 이러한 일들을 지켜 보고 여호와의 인자하심을 깨달으리로다
v.KJV: Whoso is wise, and will observe these things, even they shall understand the lovingkindness of the LORD.
v.NASB: Who is wise? Let him give heed to these things, And consider the lovingkindnesses of the LORD.
v.NIV: Let the one who is wise heed these things and ponder the loving deeds of the Lord .
v.NRSV: Let those who are wise give heed to these things, and consider the steadfast love of the LORD.
v.SAENEW: 지혜 있는 사람이 누구냐? 이 일들을 명심하고, 주님의 인자하심을 깨달아라.
v.WLB: 지혜로운 사람들과 이 일을 명심할 사람들은 여호와의 큰 사랑을 깨달을 것이다.
